seoTitle: How to Build a Habit Tracker With a Trello Plugin
metaDescription: Turn a Trello board into a habit tracker using a Power-Up plugin. Set up daily lists, log streaks, and watch your consistency in one dashboard.
intro: Trello's Kanban boards can double as a habit tracker once you add the right Power-Up plugin. Instead of downloading a separate app, you organize daily and weekly routines as cards and let a habit-tracking plugin log your completions and streaks. This walkthrough covers building the board structure and wiring up the plugin so your progress updates automatically.

steps.heading: Create the board and core lists
steps.body: Start a new Trello board and set up lists to separate daily habits, weekly rituals, and an analytics view, giving each routine type its own lane.

steps.heading: Add a habit-tracking Power-Up
steps.body: Open the board's Power-Ups (Apps) directory, search for a habit or daily tracker plugin, and add it to the board so it can attach tracking data to your cards.

steps.heading: Authorize and configure the plugin
steps.body: Open the plugin's settings icon and authorize your account so it can read and write completion data on your cards.

steps.heading: Build your first habit card
steps.body: Create a card for a specific habit, open its back panel, and enable the plugin so you can set a target goal and add checklist items for daily logging.

steps.heading: Track streaks and view your dashboard
steps.body: Mark tasks complete to populate streaks, then open the plugin's analytics dashboard to see completion charts and how consistent you've been.

faq.question: Do I need a paid Trello plan to use a habit-tracking plugin?
faq.answer: Many habit-tracking Power-Ups work on Trello's free tier, though some advanced analytics or unlimited Power-Ups per board may require a paid plan.

faq.question: Will my checkboxes reset automatically each day?
faq.answer: Some habit plugins include an automated rollover feature that refreshes checkboxes daily so you can start a fresh streak count without manual resets.

faq.question: Can I track more than one habit on the same board?
faq.answer: Yes, you can create a separate card for each habit and use labels to group them by category, like health, business, or education.

faq.question: How do I see my long-term progress?
faq.answer: The plugin's dashboard view typically includes charts that visualize your completion percentage and streak history over time.
